g.data-narration: Generic functions in C++ enable compile-time polymorphism through function templates for reusable code.
Generic programming allows writing flexible code that works with multiple data types without runtime overhead.
g.text: Generic functions (function templates) enable writing flexible, reusable code that works
with multiple data types without sacrificing performance. Achieves compile-time polymorphism.
No runtime overhead unlike virtual functions!

g.data-narration: Without templates, we need separate functions for each type. With templates, one function works for all types.
g.text: Why Do We Need Generic Functions?
✓ Single function works for ALL types

g.data-narration: The template declaration precedes the function, specifying type parameters that become placeholders.
Templates support single or multiple type parameters, default arguments, and non-type compile-time constants.
The compiler generates type-specific code at compile-time through template instantiation for zero runtime cost.
g.text: The compiler generates type-specific code at compile-time. This is called instantiation.
✓ No runtime overhead - code generated at compile time!

g.data-narration: Templates can be overloaded with regular functions. The compiler resolves calls using exact matches first.
g.text: Templates can be overloaded with regular functions or other templates. Compiler resolution order:

g.data-narration: Use template to override generic behavior for specific types when generic logic doesn't apply.
g.text: When generic template logic doesn't work for a specific type, provide custom implementation.
template<typename T> T maximum(T a, T b) { return (a>b)?a:b; }
template<> const char* maximum<const char*>(const char* a, const char* b)
template<> specifies all template parameters are explicitly provided

g.data-narration: Non-type template parameters accept compile-time constant values like integers for flexible template behavior.
g.text: Non-type parameters allow passing compile-time constants as template arguments.
T sum(T arr[]) { ... for(int i=0; i<size; i++) ... }

g.data-narration: A simple generic swap function using pass-by-reference works with any data type.
g.text: T temp = a; a = b; b = temp; }

g.data-narration: A generic findMax function iterates through arrays to find the largest element regardless of type.
g.text: for(i=1; i<n; i++) if(arr[i] > max) max = arr[i]; return max; }

g.data-narration: Templates support multiple type parameters for functions that work with different input and output types.
g.text: template<typename R, typename T1, typename T2>
R convert(T1 a, T2 b) { return static_cast<R>(a+b); }

g.data-narration: Function templates provide compile-time polymorphism without runtime overhead through generic programming.
g.text: Function templates provide compile-time polymorphism without runtime overhead
'typename' and 'class' are interchangeable in template declarations
Template arguments can be explicitly specified when deduction isn't possible
Function templates support overloading with regular functions
Use template<> for explicit specialization of specific types
